info.title: Human Service API
info.description: Scrapes and caches a person's online presence (websites, LinkedIn, blog, etc.) to provide writing context for AI content generation.

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

total.description: Total matchable. apify: pipelinelabs-only signal (provider cursor, not a cross-source-exact total).

nextOffset.description: apify offset for the next page (null when done / apollo cursor-based).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

providerPersonId.description: apollo person id (usable for a later enrich). null for apify.

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

need.description: Intent routing: 'verified_email' routes to apify. Ignored if `provider` is set.

nextPage.description: apollo only: omit filters and advance the server-managed cursor for the next page.

limit.description: apify only: max leads to return (provider cap 1000). Defaults to 1 — apify bills per returned lead (each hit carries a verified email; no free teaser list), so the gateway takes the strict minimum unless you consciously raise it to batch.

offset.description: apify only: pagination offset (pass back `nextOffset` from the prior page).

audienceId.description: Tag returned (served) leads as members of this audience (provenance membership). The audience must belong to the org (404 otherwise). Tagging applies to billed serves only — apify search hits (every hit is billed). Does not change which filters are searched; the caller supplies filters as usual.

functions.description: Job functions. Honored by apify only — apollo has no functions search filter.

companyNames.description: Company names. Honored by apify only — apollo searches by domain/industry, not name.

technologies.description: Tech stack. apify + apollo (apollo technology UIDs).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Defaults to apollo (same as search). Set 'apify' for the verified-email waterfall. The reveal follows the provider that searched — a provider person id only means something to its own provider.

providerPersonId.description: apollo only: the apollo person id returned by a prior search. Reveals the verified email via apollo /enrich (the billed path, 1 credit). PREFERRED for apollo — apollo search masks last name + domain, so identity-based match can't be satisfied from a search hit.

includeInferred.description: apify only: include pattern-inferred emails in the waterfall.

audienceId.description: Tag the resolved (served) person as a member of this audience (provenance membership). The audience must belong to the org (404 otherwise).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

audienceId.description: The id of the PERSISTED audience row (status 'suggested', inactive). The caller activates a chosen candidate via PATCH /orgs/audiences/{id}/status {status:'active'}.

name.description: Short human label for this audience (<=4 words), shared across providers — the layer-1 segment name.

rationale.description: One-sentence description of who this audience targets.

provider.description: The winning provider for this audience — the one with the larger free dry-run count (tie -> apollo). Only this provider's filters/count are returned.

count.description: The winning provider's live match count (free dry-run). 0 means the LLM could not find a non-empty, valid filter set within the iteration budget on either provider.

validationError.description: Set when the winning provider rejected the filters as invalid after the iteration budget was exhausted.

truncated.description: Reserved for response compatibility. Current Layer 1 has no hard cap, so freshly suggested candidates return false.

nlPrompt.description: Natural-language audience description. The LLM reads the caller's own granularity intent from this text (e.g. 'split by country', 'founders in FR and DE separately') and emits one candidate per implied segment — granularity and count are NOT separate inputs.

description.description: One-sentence summary of who THIS audience targets, distinct from the shared batch nlPrompt. LLM-generated at /suggest time. null for rows predating this field.

avatarUrl.description: Audience avatar as a self-contained data: URI (image bytes generated by chat-service via POST /orgs/audiences/{id}/avatar). null until generated.

provider.description: The provider this audience commits to, when persisting a candidate from /suggest. Omit for a neutral audience.

apolloCount.description: Optional count snapshot the caller already obtained from /orgs/people/search/dry-run (apollo). Stored as-is; refresh-count re-computes it server-side later. Accepts null (e.g. an apify-source candidate carries apolloCount: null).

apifyCount.description: Optional apify count snapshot (see apolloCount). Accepts null (e.g. an apollo-source candidate carries apifyCount: null).

status.description: 'served' ⟹ a fresh person is returned. 'exhausted' ⟹ no new match remains for this audience within the suppression window (person is null).

allOf.description: The next unserved person (real provider match on the audience's stored filters), recorded as served so the next call returns someone new. null when exhausted.

prompt.description: Optional image prompt override (the dashboard AI-chat tool can steer the avatar). Omitted ⟹ chat-service is prompted from the audience's own descriptors.

scanned.description: Audiences with description IS NULL found by this sweep.

wouldBackfill.description: Null-description audiences that would be backfilled (= scanned).

backfilled.description: Audiences whose description was generated + written (0 on a dry-run).

failed.description: Rows whose LLM generation failed (left null, retried on re-run).

sample.description: Per-audience preview (capped): {id,name,description} — description is null on a dry-run, the generated sentence on a real run.

remapped.description: Audiences whose filters were translated (0 on a dry-run).

wouldRemap.description: Audiences that still hold persona vocab and would be translated.

scanned.description: Non-deprecated apify audiences inspected by this sweep.

wouldMigrate.description: Apify audiences that would be migrated (= scanned on a dry-run).

migrated.description: Per-audience result: the deprecated apify id, the new active apollo id (mirrored status), and the apollo count from the re-derived filters. Empty on a dry-run.

failed.description: Apify audiences whose apollo re-derivation yielded no usable filter set (left untouched, retried on re-run).

sample.description: Per-audience preview (capped): {id,name,status} of the apify rows that would migrate.

started.description: Present (true) only on an async run — the sweep runs in the background.

scanned.description: Live audiences (status<>'deprecated') with avatar_url IS NULL.

wouldFill.description: Audiences that would get an avatar (= scanned on a dry-run).

filled.description: Audiences whose avatar was generated + stored (0 on a dry-run / async).

failed.description: Audiences whose image generation failed (transient chat error); left null, retried on re-run.

sample.description: Per-audience preview (capped) of rows that would be filled.

post.summary: One-time data fix: generate a per-audience one-sentence description (from name + filters via chat-service) for every audience whose description is null (idempotent, dry-runnable)

schema.description: When 'true', count null-description audiences + return an {id,name} sample WITHOUT calling the LLM or writing. Defaults to false (real run).

parameters.description: When 'true', count null-description audiences + return an {id,name} sample WITHOUT calling the LLM or writing. Defaults to false (real run).
parameters.name: dryRun

post.summary: One-time data fix: translate backfilled audiences' filters from legacy persona vocab to the canonical PeopleSearchFilters vocab, in place (idempotent, dry-runnable, reversible)

schema.description: When 'true', report counts + a before/after sample without writing. Defaults to false (real run).

parameters.description: When 'true', report counts + a before/after sample without writing. Defaults to false (real run).
parameters.name: dryRun

post.summary: One-time data fix: for every non-deprecated apify audience, re-derive an equivalent apollo filter set (agentic refine, platform LLM), create a new apollo audience mirroring the source status, and mark the apify one 'deprecated' (idempotent, dry-runnable, reversible)

schema.description: When 'true', scan the non-deprecated apify audiences + return a sample WITHOUT calling the LLM/apollo or writing. Defaults to false (real run).

parameters.description: When 'true', scan the non-deprecated apify audiences + return a sample WITHOUT calling the LLM/apollo or writing. Defaults to false (real run).
parameters.name: dryRun

schema.description: When 'true', respond 202 immediately and run the sweep in the background (each row's agentic refine can exceed an HTTP timeout; a whole-table run certainly does). Progress is durable per-row + observable via ?dryRun=true. Ignored when dryRun=true.

parameters.description: When 'true', respond 202 immediately and run the sweep in the background (each row's agentic refine can exceed an HTTP timeout; a whole-table run certainly does). Progress is durable per-row + observable via ?dryRun=true. Ignored when dryRun=true.
parameters.name: async

post.summary: One-time data fix: generate + store a flat-vector avatar for every live audience whose avatar_url is null, via chat-service's org-less platform image path (no org billing, no user needed) — idempotent, dry-runnable, async

schema.description: When 'true', count the live audiences missing an avatar + return a sample WITHOUT calling chat-service or writing. Defaults to false (real run).

parameters.description: When 'true', count the live audiences missing an avatar + return a sample WITHOUT calling chat-service or writing. Defaults to false (real run).
parameters.name: dryRun

schema.description: When 'true', respond 202 immediately and run the sweep in the background (image generation is slow; a whole-table run exceeds an HTTP timeout). Progress is durable per-row + observable via ?dryRun=true. Ignored when dryRun=true.

parameters.description: When 'true', respond 202 immediately and run the sweep in the background (image generation is slow; a whole-table run exceeds an HTTP timeout). Progress is durable per-row + observable via ?dryRun=true. Ignored when dryRun=true.
parameters.name: async
